label: Incident Report
description: Formal incident report with witness statements, damage assessment, and recommended actions.
generation_notes:
Voice: Incident-report formality — objective, chronological, factual. Written as if for legal/insurance review.

Structure:
- Incident Summary (one paragraph: what, when, where, who)
- Detailed Description (chronological narrative, specific times and actions)
- Immediate Response (what was done at the scene)
- Witnesses (listed if any)
- Damage Assessment (factual, avoid speculation)
- Photos (reference embedded images if provided)
- Root Cause Hypothesis (clearly labeled as preliminary if stated)
- Recommended Actions (specific, assignable)
- Reporter Attestation

Tone examples:
  CORRECT: "At 14:27, water began discharging from the overhead sprinkler in Conference Room B. Building occupants were evacuated by 14:35. Fire suppression was contacted at 14:29."
  WRONG: "It was a really scary situation when the sprinklers suddenly went off and everyone was in a panic!"

Do not assign fault. Do not speculate beyond what evidence supports. Use past tense exclusively.

Length target: 400-800 words depending on incident complexity.
